characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The Hot Shot, All-American Earthling,de factoleader of the kids. He is skilled at gymnastics and martial arts. Actor Allusion: In "All You Can Eaty," he refers to the bridge as the "command center" (the only timeanyonecalls it that) — um,wrong show, Walter! All There in the Manual: Back when there was an official website, his profile said that he hailed from the island of Los Angeles. This never comes up during the show. Badass Normal: As the only bog-standard human among the kids, he's also the only one without any "powers", though his martial arts prowess helps make up for it. Belligerent Sexual Tension: His interactions with Catalinaand later Suzeeusually come off as this.

characters.name: The Teachers
characters.description: The kids' long-suffering teacher at the Academy, who accidentally gets stuck on board the ship after having to go search for them when they sneak out of her class onto theChrista. Const Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Commander Goddard. Butt-Monkey: Receives the most (comedic) abuse and misfortune out of anybody in the entire show. Crouching Moron, Hidden Badass: On the veryveryrare occasions she avoids fainting or panicking, she can be surprisingly effective. Fainting: Near constantly.

characters.name: The Spung
characters.description: A young Spung Oracle girl who runs away from her people to escape their control over their women, and ends up meeting and helping the Christa's crew.Was going to eventually become a main character,but the network kiboshed itandshoved inSuzee instead. Green-Skinned Space Babe: A green, reptilian but otherwise humanoid girl. Interspecies Romance: With Radu, eventually. Mad Scientist's Beautiful Daughter Non-Mammalian Mammaries: She's a lizard woman with curly hair and breasts. Don't ask too many questions.

characters.name: Everyone Else
characters.description: Catalina's "imaginary" friend, who turns out to really be an alien girl living in a different dimension. All There in the Manual: The long-defunct official website listed her homeworld as "Yensid". Astral Projection: She is able to project her mind into other people's bodies temporarily. Insufferable Genius: Her whole species (whatever they are) are geniuses. Granted she'smuchless frustrating to be around than Catalina due to being considerably more focused on the tasks at hand rather than flaunting her talents. Not-So-Imaginary Friend: Everybody else thinks she's imaginary, but at the end of Season 1 she turns out to be real.
